name: Google Translate
summary: Translate a string to a target language using Google's inline translate widget.
description: Translate text using Google's Search translate widget (best-effort). Avoids the full Translate SPA selectors.

text.description: Text to translate. Keep it short — the widget is meant for phrases/sentences, not paragraphs.

to_language.description: Target language NAME (Spanish, Japanese, French, German, Italian). Use the full name — ISO codes (es, ja) often fail to trigger Google's translate widget.

steps.name: Navigate to Google Search translate widget
Extract translation (#tw-target-text is the canonical widget output)

help.summary: Translate a string to a target language using Google's inline translate widget.

parameters.text: Text to translate. Keep it short — the widget is meant for phrases/sentences, not paragraphs.
parameters.to_language: Target language NAME (Spanish, Japanese, French, German, Italian). Use the full name — ISO codes (es, ja) often fail to trigger Google's translate widget.

examples.command: rzn-browser run google translate --param text="good morning" --param to_language=Spanish
rzn-browser run google translate --param text="thank you" --param to_language=Japanese

returns: Object with translated_text (from #tw-target-text), source_text (from #tw-source-text), and search_text (full #search panel as fallback). If translated_text is empty, the widget didn't render — try a language NAME, not a code.
notes: Source language is auto-detected by Google.
For long-form translation or document upload, use the Translate SPA directly — this workflow only targets the Search widget.
